page.title: Measles — Disease Intelligence | OSMF
page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for Measles: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for Measles: 24 alterations and 11 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

alterations.name: Lymphadenopathy
Antibody titres
Concentrations of Anti-circumsporozoite Protein (Anti-CS) Antibodies.
Concentrations of Antibodies Against Hepatitis B (Anti-HB Antibodies).
Growth
Microscopy assessment of the infectious activity of the measles vaccine virus presented in breast milk in at least one sample.
Morbidity
Mortality
Number of Participants With Postvaccination Mumps ELISA Antibody Titer ≥10 Ab Units/mL
Number of Participants With Postvaccination Rubella ELISA Antibody Titer ≥10 IU/mL
Number of Participants With Unsolicited AEs
Number of Subjects With Anti-hepatitis A Virus (HAV) Antibody Concentration Equal or Above the Cut-off Value in HAV and HAV+MMR+V Groups
Number of Subjects With Serious Adverse Events (SAEs).
Number of Subjects With Unsolicited Adverse Events (AEs)
Number of infants with reported clinical symptoms of measles
Number of women with positive RT-PCR for measles vaccine virus strain in urine in at least one sample
Number of women with reported clinical symptoms of measles
Phase A: Number of Subjects Reporting Fever
Prevalence of the measles vaccine virus strain in breast milk.
Proportion of participants with protective antibodies to measles, mumps, and rubella
Quantification of the measles vaccine virus strain presented in breast milk in at least one sample
The geometric means of anti-measles IgG level
The geometric means of anti-mumps IgG titers
The geometric means of anti-rubella IgG level

disclaimer: Alterations and therapeutics are aggregated from public databases (Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, DGIdb, and others). Natural agents are candidates from OSMF narrative reviews on opensourcemed.info/chronic-disease.html — not approved treatments. This page is for research orientation only — not medical advice.
